name: Obi
description: A boisterous young Labrador with a golden coat and boundless energy.
{pronounSubjectCap} fixes you with intelligent amber eyes, tail wagging furiously.

{pronounPossessiveCap} collar tag reads:

I run ahead but never leave.
I guard the path though I cannot speak.
Where the loyal wait and the lost find home,
Seek the place where two paths become one.
On the reverse side, worn almost smooth with time, are four faint symbols:
<ul>
• A paw.
• A heart.
• A ball.
• A compass.
{pronounSubjectCap} seems to be waiting for something, or someone.
talkTemplates: Obi stares at you with {pronounPossessive} head tilted to one side, clearly delighted by the attention.
Obi responds to your words with a single, very decisive bark.
Obi wags {pronounPossessive} tail so hard {pronounPossessive} whole back end swings. {pronounSubjectCap} has no idea what you said, but {pronounSubject} seems thrilled you said it.
Obi licks your hand enthusiastically. Probably not the response you were looking for.
Obi sits very straight and fixes you with an attentive gaze, as though {pronounSubject} understands every single word. {pronounSubjectCap} does not.
Obi's ears flatten and {pronounSubject} lets out a soft whine, gazing up at you with enormous amber eyes.
Obi nudges your hand with {pronounPossessive} cold nose and looks up expectantly. The conversation is going well, from {pronounPossessive} perspective.
interactTemplates: Obi's head snaps up the moment {player} enters. {pronounPossessiveCap} tail becomes a golden blur.
Obi spots {player} and immediately trots over, sniffing their boots with great enthusiasm.
Obi lets out a short, happy bark as {player} walks in, then circles them twice.
Obi lifts {pronounPossessive} head from {pronounPossessive} paws and watches {player} with bright, hopeful eyes.
Obi scrambles to {pronounPossessive} feet and presses {pronounPossessive} cold nose into {player}'s hand.
At the sight of {player}, Obi's entire back end wiggles furiously.
Obi yawns an enormous yawn, stretches, then pads over to greet {player} as if they've been gone for years.

departureTemplates: Obi's ears prick up â€” {pronounSubject} sniffs the air and trots off to the {dir}.
Obi spots something interesting and bolts {dir} before you can blink.
Obi gives you an apologetic tail-wag, then pads off to the {dir}.
Obi suddenly barks at nothing in particular and disappears to the {dir}.
With a wag of {pronounPossessive} tail, Obi trots {dir} and is gone.
Obi gets distracted by something you can't see and scampers off to the {dir}.
Obi sneezes, looks embarrassed, then trots off to the {dir} with great purpose.
Something catches Obi's eye to the {dir} and Obi is off like a shot.
arrivalTemplates: Obi bounds in from the {from}, tongue lolling, tail spinning furiously.
A familiar golden blur skids in from the {from} â€” it's Obi, looking very pleased with {pronounReflexive}.
Obi trots in from the {from} and immediately sniffs everything in sight.
Obi pads in from the {from}, ears perked, eyes bright.
Obi charges in from the {from} and nearly knocks you over in excitement.
Obi saunters in from the {from}, tail held high, acting as though {pronounSubject} owns the place.
There's a clatter from the {from} and then Obi appears, utterly unrepentant.

name: Keeper of the Bond
description:
A silent knight stands watch beside the ancient stone of the grove. {pronounPossessiveCap} armour is old but immaculately kept, etched with the faint image of a hound's paw upon the breastplate. A long cloak hangs from {pronounPossessive} shoulders, unmoving despite the breeze. Though {pronounPossessive} face is hidden behind a simple steel helm, you feel the unmistakable weight of {pronounPossessive} attention.

When {pronounSubject} finally speaks, {pronounPossessive} voice is calm and steady.

"Many claim loyalty. Few understand it."

{pronounSubjectCap} gestures toward the path beyond the grove as though inviting you to prove something.
talkTemplates: Loyalty is not claimed. It is proven.
The Keeper of the Bond regards you silently for a long moment before speaking. "Loyalty is not sworn. It is shown."
"Many speak of loyalty," the Keeper says quietly. "Few remain when it is tested."
The Keeper rests a gauntleted hand on the pommel of {pronounPossessive} sword. "Would you stand when another depends on you?"
The Keeper tilts {pronounPossessive} head slightly. "The faithful do not ask what they gain."
"Once," the Keeper says softly, "there was a hound who understood loyalty better than kings."
The Keeper watches you with quiet patience, as though weighing your character rather than your strength.
"The world was shaped by great hands," the Keeper says. "But even the maker walked beside a companion."
interactTemplates: The Keeper of the Bond shifts slightly as {player} approaches, armour softly creaking.
The Keeper inclines {pronounPossessive} head respectfully toward {player}.
The Keeper studies {player} in silence, as though measuring something unseen.
The Keeper stands unmoving as {player} enters the grove.
The Keeper turns {pronounPossessive} gaze toward {player}, {pronounPossessive} presence calm but imposing.
The Keeper folds {pronounPossessive} arms across {pronounPossessive} chest and waits patiently.
The Keeper nods once toward {player}, acknowledging their presence.

name: Waystation Caretaker
description:
A broad-shouldered warden wrapped in a weather-faded cloak stands beside the cold watchfire brazier. Soot stains {pronounPossessive} hands and sleeves, and {pronounPossessive} tired eyes keep drifting toward the ledge above the trail.

"The light went out three nights ago," comes the weary explanation. "If the old ember lantern still rests above, we may yet guide the lost home."
talkTemplates: The caretaker rubs ash between {pronounPossessive} fingers. "This trail used to shine all night. Now the dark takes travelers before dawn."
"The lantern should be somewhere on the prayer ledge," the caretaker says. "I am too old to scramble up there in this wind."
The caretaker glances toward the grove below. "The Keeper sent me here when the old road began to fail. I have kept watch ever since."
"Bring me the ember lantern and I can wake the beacon again," the caretaker says. "That is all I ask."
The caretaker nods toward the brazier. "A road is only useful if someone tends the light."
interactTemplates: The caretaker straightens as {player} approaches, one soot-dark hand resting on the empty brazier.
The caretaker looks up from the cold beacon and gives {player} a weary nod.
A gust rattles the waystation timbers as the caretaker turns toward {player}.
The caretaker brushes ash from {pronounPossessive} cloak and studies {player} hopefully.

name: Restless Undead Wayfarer
description: A traveler-shaped shade draped in torn road-cloth, {pronounPossessive} face half-lost behind a sheen of grave-cold mist. Blue fire leaks from the cracks in {pronounPossessive} ribs each time {pronounSubject} stirs.
talkTemplates: The shade answers with a wet rattle, as if water is sloshing in lungs {pronounSubject} no longer possesses.
A whisper slips out of the dead thing, thin as winter wind. {pronounSubjectCap} is not speaking any language you know.
The restless wayfarer tilts {pronounPossessive} head, empty eyes burning brighter for a heartbeat.
interactTemplates: The restless wayfarer drifts toward {player}, leaving frost across the stone.
A dead traveler's silhouette turns as {player} approaches, ember-blue eyes flaring.
The shade's ruined cloak stirs though there is no wind near it.

name: Ashen Undead Pilgrim
description: A prayer-worn corpse wrapped in charred ribbons and shrine ash. {pronounPossessiveCap} hands are locked in a supplicant pose until {pronounSubject} lunges, leaving a trail of soot behind {pronounObject}.
talkTemplates: Ash dribbles from the pilgrim's jaw as {pronounSubject} opens {pronounPossessive} mouth in a soundless plea.
The dead pilgrim bows once, then jerks upright with a hiss like a dying coal.
Nothing living answers from the pilgrim, only the dry scrape of bone against cloth.
interactTemplates: The ashen pilgrim lurches toward {player}, ribbons snapping like burnt paper.
Soot swirls around the pilgrim's feet as {pronounSubject} drags {pronounReflexive} closer to {player}.
The pilgrim raises {pronounPossessive} head toward {player}, eyes glowing like buried coals.

name: Drill Instructor Magnus
description: A battle-scarred veteran with a shaved head and arms like tree trunks. {pronounPossessiveCap} voice carries across the yard like a thunderclap. Despite {pronounPossessive} gruff exterior, there's a patient wisdom in {pronounPossessive} eyes â€” {pronounSubject} {pronounHave} trained hundreds of recruits, and {pronounSubject} will train hundreds more.
talkTemplates: Magnus crosses {pronounPossessive} arms and studies you. "Want to learn to fight? First, learn to survive. Attack those dummies until your arms burn."
"Combat isn't about strength alone," Magnus growls. "It's about knowing when to strike and when to defend."
Magnus snorts. "You think a real enemy will wait? Practice your attacks on those dummies until it becomes instinct."
"To attack, type attack followed by your target," Magnus explains. "Try it on a training dummy â€” they don't hit back. Much."
Magnus nods approvingly. "Good. You're here to learn. Hit the dummies, watch your stamina, and remember: a tired fighter is a dead fighter."
"Every great warrior started right here," Magnus says, gesturing at the training yard. "Show me what you've got."
Magnus cracks his knuckles. "Combat is simple: attack your enemy before they attack you. These dummies are perfect for practice."
interactTemplates: Magnus eyes {player} critically as they enter the training yard. "Fresh meat. Let's see what you're made of."
Magnus gives {player} a curt nod. "Ready to train? The dummies aren't going to hit themselves."
Magnus pauses his inspection of a practice sword as {player} approaches. "Another recruit. Good."
Magnus turns his attention to {player}. "If you're here to learn, grab a weapon and start swinging."
Magnus crosses his arms as {player} enters. "The training yard is open to all. Don't waste my time."

name: Straw Training Dummy
description: A man-shaped figure stuffed with straw, mounted on a wooden post. {pronounPossessiveCap} burlap face is painted with a crude, menacing expression. Deep gashes and sword cuts cover {pronounPossessive} torso â€” evidence of countless practice sessions. A sign hangs around {pronounPossessive} neck: "Attack me to practice combat!"
talkTemplates: The training dummy stares back at you with cold, painted eyes. It says nothing. It's a dummy.
You address the straw dummy. It does not respond. Unsurprising, really.
The dummy sways slightly in the breeze. If it understood you, it shows no sign.
Talking to a training dummy. Bold strategy. It remains unmoved by your words.
The dummy's painted smile seems to mock your attempt at conversation.
interactTemplates: The straw dummy stands motionless, waiting to be struck.
A gentle breeze causes the training dummy to sway slightly on its post.
The training dummy's burlap face stares vacantly as {player} passes by.
Bits of straw poke out from fresh cuts in the dummy's torso.

name: Armored Training Dummy
description: A reinforced training dummy wearing dented iron plates strapped over {pronounPossessive} straw body. This one is clearly meant for practicing against armored opponents. The metal is scratched and battered from countless blows.
talkTemplates: The armored dummy clanks slightly as you speak to it. Still no response.
Your words echo off the iron plates. The dummy remains stoically silent.
You try to engage the armored dummy in conversation. It's about as responsive as you'd expect.
The armored dummy's iron faceplate stares back at you, unmoved by your eloquence.
interactTemplates: The armored dummy stands firm, its iron plates glinting in the light.
A dull metallic rattle sounds as the wind catches the armored dummy's plates.
The armored dummy's dented armor tells tales of countless practice battles.

name: Spinning Training Dummy
description: An advanced training mechanism â€” a wooden mannequin mounted on a swivel with weighted arms that spin when struck. One wrong move and those arms will swing back at you. A plaque reads: "Hit it, then dodge!"
talkTemplates: The spinning dummy creaks ominously. It has no ears, so your words fall on... wood.
You talk to the spinning dummy. Its weighted arms sway gently, as if shrugging.
The wooden mechanism groans but offers no verbal response. How rude.
Speaking to training equipment. Bold. The dummy spins lazily in response.
interactTemplates: The spinning dummy rattles gently, its weighted arms swaying in anticipation.
A slight breeze sets the spinning dummy's arms rotating slowly.
The spinning dummy creaks on its swivel, ready for action.

name: Lost Traveler
description:
A weary figure huddles against a tree, {pronounPossessive} traveling cloak torn and muddy. Nervous eyes dart at every forest sound, and relief floods {pronounPossessive} face the moment you draw near.

"Please â€” I've been lost for days. I can't find my way back to town."
talkTemplates: The traveler clutches your arm gratefully. "You'll help me? Truly? I thought I'd die out here alone."
"I was following the road when the mist came," the traveler says. "Before I knew it, I was hopelessly turned around."
The traveler's voice breaks. "My family must be so worried. I just want to go home."
"The forest plays tricks," the traveler whispers. "Paths that lead nowhere. Sounds that aren't there."
The traveler nods eagerly. "West. I remember now â€” town is to the west. Will you walk with me?"
"I had this once," the traveler says, pressing something brass into your hand. "A keepsake from better days. Take it â€” for your kindness."
interactTemplates: The lost traveler looks up hopefully as {player} approaches.
The traveler wrings {pronounPossessive} hands nervously, watching {player}.
The traveler takes a tentative step toward {player}, uncertainty in {pronounPossessive} eyes.
A flicker of hope crosses the traveler's face at the sight of {player}.

name: Wounded Pilgrim
description:
A pilgrim in simple robes lies propped against a rock, one hand pressed against a bloody wound in {pronounPossessive} side. {pronounPossessiveCap} face is pale but {pronounPossessive} eyes are calm.

"Fear not for me, traveler. The wound looks worse than it is. But... I could use aid, if you're willing to offer it."
talkTemplates: The pilgrim smiles weakly. "Kindness costs nothing but courage. Remember that."
"I was set upon by wolves," the pilgrim explains. "I outran them, but not their teeth."
The pilgrim studies your face. "You have a good heart. I can see it."
"A healing potion would mend this wound," the pilgrim admits. "But I won't beg for what isn't freely given."
The pilgrim nods gratefully. "To help a stranger when you could walk past â€” that is true courage."
The pilgrim presses a brass fragment into your palm. "I found this on the road. It feels... important. Perhaps it was meant for someone braver than me."

name: Joyful Child
description:
A young child sits on the fountain's edge, swinging {pronounPossessive} legs and humming a cheerful tune. A worn leather ball rests in {pronounPossessive} lap, and bright eyes keep darting around in search of someone to play with.

"Hey! You look fun! Wanna play catch? Please please please?"
talkTemplates: The child bounces excitedly. "If you find my lucky ball, I'll give you something special! I lost it somewhere in the market!"
"Games are the best!" the child declares. "My grandma says even heroes need to play sometimes."
The child giggles. "Do you know any dogs? I love dogs! They always want to play!"
"I found this shiny thing once," the child says, pulling a brass fragment from {pronounPossessive} pocket. "But I'd trade it for my ball!"
The child's eyes light up. "You'll help me find it? You're the BEST!"
"Grown-ups forget how to have fun," the child says sagely. "Don't be like that, okay?"
interactTemplates: The child waves enthusiastically as {player} walks by.
The child hops off the fountain and runs over to {player}.
The child tugs at {player}'s sleeve. "Play with me?"
The child beams at {player}, practically vibrating with energy.

name: Clerk Elin
description:
A brisk clerk in ink-smudged sleeves stands behind a stack of ledgers almost as tall as the desk itself. {pronounPossessiveCap} expression suggests the same question has already been asked six times today, but {pronounSubject} still offers a professional smile.

"If you need records, permits, or directions, speak clearly and one at a time."
talkTemplates: Elin taps a ledger. "Most problems in town begin as paperwork and end as twice as much paperwork."
"The market complains, the watch complains, and the mayor complains about both," Elin says. "So the day proceeds normally."
Elin adjusts a stack of notices. "If you're headed beyond the gate, sign the road book first. Captain Hale insists."
"Town Hall keeps the place stitched together, more or less," Elin says. "Try not to pull on any loose thread."
interactTemplates: Clerk Elin glances up from a ledger as {player} approaches.
Elin straightens a pile of notices and gives {player} an efficient nod.
The town clerk pauses mid-entry to acknowledge {player}.

name: Banker Merrow
description:
A careful, silver-haired banker watches the room over half-moon spectacles, one hand always resting near the day's ledger key. {pronounPossessiveCap} coat is modest, but every seam suggests precise taste and older money.

"Coin is only trust stamped in metal. Spend either carelessly and you soon go hungry."
talkTemplates: Merrow folds {pronounPossessive} hands. "Harvest loans, rebuilding loans, winter advances. A town survives because people trust tomorrow enough to borrow from it."
"The road to the forest takes more coin from this town than bandits ever did," Merrow murmurs. "Repairs are expensive. So are funerals."
Merrow inclines {pronounPossessive} head. "If you carry valuables, remember that confidence is not the same thing as security."
"Captain Hale worries about blades. I worry about roofs, grain, and debt," Merrow says. "All three can ruin a family."
interactTemplates: Banker Merrow closes a ledger softly as {player} nears the counter.
Merrow's attention shifts to {player} with measured calm.
The banker studies {player} as though weighing risk against promise.

name: Captain Hale
description:
A broad-shouldered watch captain in a weather-creased cloak stands in the barracks yard, reading the road reports with a soldier's patience and a parent's fatigue. Old scars vanish into {pronounPossessive} collar.

"If you're heading outside the walls, keep your eyes up and your route simple. The wild only surprises fools once."
talkTemplates: Hale jerks {pronounPossessive} chin toward the gate. "We keep the road open. Most days, that's the same as keeping people alive."
"The forest isn't evil," Hale says. "It just doesn't care whether you come back out. Plan accordingly."
Hale taps the duty board. "Half the work is patrols. The other half is convincing townsfolk they don't need three escorts to visit a cousin."
"If trouble starts inside the walls, I hear of it by noon. If it starts outside the walls, I hear of it when someone limps home," Hale says grimly.
interactTemplates: Captain Hale looks up from the watch board as {player} crosses the yard.
Hale gives {player} the quick once-over of someone used to measuring readiness.
The captain folds the latest road report and turns to {player}.

name: Innkeeper Mira
description:
A sharp-eyed innkeeper in a rolled-sleeve apron moves through the common-room with practiced ease, carrying plates, keeping the peace, and remembering exactly who still owes for supper.

"Roof, stew, and a clean cup cost less than trouble. I recommend the first three."
talkTemplates: Mira wipes down the bar. "Everyone comes through the Flagon eventually. Farmers, guards, traders, wanderers, liars. Usually in that order."
"If you want news, sit near the hearth. If you want silence, rent the loft," Mira says. "If you want both, you're out of luck."
Mira nods toward the bakery. "Tomas sends over the morning loaves before sunrise. That's how I know the world hasn't ended in the night."
"You're welcome to a room, but no muddy boots on the blankets," Mira says, not entirely joking.
interactTemplates: Innkeeper Mira looks up from the hearth as {player} enters.
Mira slides a cup aside and greets {player} with a brisk nod.
The innkeeper catches sight of {player} and clears a space at the bar.

name: Baker Tomas
description:
A flour-dusted baker with strong forearms and a permanently warm face moves between the oven and the cooling racks as if {pronounSubject} {pronounHave} been doing it since childhood. Which, judging by the confidence in {pronounPossessive} hands, {pronounSubject} probably {pronounHave}.

"Fresh bread forgives a lot. Not everything, but a lot."
talkTemplates: Tomas laughs softly. "The trick is heat, patience, and not panicking when the dough looks wrong. Same as most of life."
"If the square smells good at dawn, that's me," Tomas says with clear pride.
Tomas jerks a thumb toward the inn. "Mira buys first pick every morning. Says a good loaf keeps the peace better than a club."
"People talk over bread more honestly than over coin," Tomas says. "That's how you learn a town."
interactTemplates: Baker Tomas looks up from a peel of fresh loaves as {player} approaches.
Tomas dusts flour from {pronounPossessive} hands and smiles toward {player}.
The baker shifts a tray off the rack and nods to {player}.

name: Shopkeeper Rona
description:
A practical merchant with measuring twine looped around one wrist keeps the general store in immaculate order. {pronounSubjectCap} {pronounHave} the gaze of someone who can tell at once whether you need lamp oil, rope, or better judgment.

"If I don't stock it, you probably don't need it before supper. Probably."
talkTemplates: Rona taps the nearest crate. "Needles, cord, salt, soap, lantern hooks. Town runs on small things more than grand ones."
"People remember the swordsmith," Rona says. "They forget the person who sells them lamp wicks and winter thread. That is as it should be."
Rona smiles thinly. "If you're headed past the gate, buy rope before you decide you can improvise without it."
"I hear every complaint in town eventually," Rona says. "Usually while wrapping someone's parcel."
interactTemplates: Shopkeeper Rona glances up from the counter as {player} enters the store.
Rona adjusts a row of lanterns, then turns to {player}.
The shopkeeper gives {player} a measuring look and a courteous nod.

name: Blacksmith Bram
description:
A soot-dark blacksmith with forearms like hammered iron works the forge with steady, economical force. Sparks leap around {pronounObject}, but {pronounSubject} scarcely seems to notice.

"Steel doesn't care what you intend. Only what you do to it."
talkTemplates: Bram eyes the blade on {pronounPossessive} anvil. "Every weapon tells you when you've rushed it. Usually by failing at the worst possible time."
"Magnus trains people to swing," Bram says. "I make sure what they're swinging survives the lesson."
Bram grunts toward the market. "Coin comes from the square. Good ore comes from patience. I prefer the second."
"If your gear rattles, slips, or bends, bring it back before it gets you killed," Bram says.
interactTemplates: Blacksmith Bram looks up from the anvil as {player} steps into the forge.
Bram sets down his hammer and studies {player} through the sparks.
The blacksmith wipes soot from {pronounPossessive} brow and acknowledges {player}.

name: Relicsmith Elira
description:
A silver-haired relicsmith works at a sidebench crowded with jeweler's files, tiny tongs, and shallow brass trays. Older marks than the forge-fire itself scar {pronounPossessive} apron, and a blue spirit flame dances in the cup of a narrow lamp beside {pronounObject}.

"Bring me honest metal and a fair fee," {pronounSubject} says without looking up, "and I'll see whether it remembers the shape it was meant to keep."
talkTemplates: "Old oathwork doesn't answer to brute force," Elira says. "It answers to heat, patience, and a hand that knows what promise the metal was made to hold."
"If you've gathered the four brass shards tied to Obi's name, bring me one of them when the set is complete," Elira says. "Hand me the work and 60 gold, and I'll reforge the tag."
Elira turns a fragment of brass beneath the lamp. "Most relics want polishing. The good ones want remembering."
"Four shards, one tag," Elira murmurs. "That much I can mend. What wakes after that is between you and the oath."

alreadyOwnedDialogue: "The tag is already whole," Elira says. "If the oath will answer you now, it will answer at the stone."
missingRequiredItemsDialogue: "I can mend Obi's tag, but I need all four shards on my bench at once," Elira says. "Bring me the full set."
insufficientGoldDialogue: "The brass is ready, but the work will cost {costGold} gold," Elira says. "Come back when you can pay for the fire and the craft."
successDialogue: Elira sets the four shards upon the anvil and arranges them with ritual care. As her fingers leave the metal, the forge flares bright of its own accord.
White fire coils around the fragments. The workshop falls still. Even the bellows seem to hold their breath.
The sound rings out like a bell through a chapel.

A second blow follows, and light spills through the cracks in the shards like molten gold. Faint shapes flicker within the glow -- a paw, a heart, a winding road, the ghost of a ball tumbling through sunlit grass.
A third and final blow falls.
The broken pieces lift from the anvil and draw together, not as metal forced into shape, but as something long separated at last made whole.
When the light fades, a single shining tag remains.
{name} threads the finished piece onto fresh leather and places {rewardItem} gently in your hand. The brass is warm, and for the briefest moment, it seems to pulse.
"Some things are forged," {name} murmurs. "And some are returned."
giveInteractions.missingRewardItemMessage: "The work is ready, but the finished tag isn't where I left it," Elira says, frowning at the bench.

interactTemplates: Elira glances up from her sidebench as {player} nears, blue flame reflecting in her spectacles.
The relicsmith brushes brass filings from {pronounPossessive} apron and studies {player} with measured curiosity.
Elira sets down a pair of tongs and gives {player} a small, professional nod.

name: Apothecary Selin
description:
A narrow-eyed apothecary in a moss-green shawl sorts dried herbs with delicate precision. Every jar on the shelf is labeled in the same exact hand, and every smell in the room seems to come with a warning.

"Medicine is only poison with a better reputation and a smaller dose."
talkTemplates: Selin ties off a bundle of herbs. "Most ailments are boring. Most cures are bitter. People remain shocked by both facts."
"Health potions mend flesh, not foolishness," Selin says. "Be careful what you expect them to solve."
Selin glances toward the road. "The forest provides half my stock and most of my worries."
"If something glows, stings, or smells sweet in the wilds, assume it wants respect," Selin advises.
interactTemplates: Apothecary Selin looks up from {pronounPossessive} herb bundles as {player} enters.
Selin corks a vial and turns {pronounPossessive} attention to {player}.
The apothecary watches {player} with thoughtful reserve.

name: Stablehand Elsie
description:
A wiry stablehand with hay in {pronounPossessive} hair and mud on {pronounPossessive} boots moves easily between the stalls, calming horses with a touch and a word. {pronounSubjectCap} smells of leather, oats, and open weather.

"A horse knows before you do whether the road ahead is a good idea. Shame riders don't listen half as well."
talkTemplates: Elsie pats a mare's neck. "Road horses hate the east wind. Means rain or worse. Usually both."
"If you're sleeping at the inn and leaving at dawn, tell Mira to wake you before I start saddling," Elsie says.
Elsie jerks {pronounPossessive} head toward the gate. "Everything that leaves town passes me first. Wagons, patrols, bad ideas."
"This stable hears more secrets than Town Hall," Elsie says. "Horses never repeat them."
interactTemplates: Stablehand Elsie glances over a stall door as {player} enters the yard.
Elsie brushes hay from {pronounPossessive} sleeves and nods to {player}.
A gelding snorts softly while Elsie looks toward {player}.

name: Keeper Sera
description:
A quiet temple keeper in layered grey-blue robes tends the court candles with patient hands. {pronounPossessiveCap} voice is low, but it carries with the same calm steadiness as the bells above the eaves.

"Not every prayer asks for rescue. Some only ask to be remembered."
talkTemplates: Sera lights another candle. "The old district remembers longer than the rest of town cares to. That is both burden and gift."
"The ruined shrine still draws offerings," Sera says. "Faith rarely leaves just because the roof does."
Sera inclines {pronounPossessive} head. "If you walk the graveyard, do so gently. Grief lingers even where bodies do not."
"Most travelers ask for safety," Sera says softly. "Most towns ask for endurance."
interactTemplates: Keeper Sera looks up from the prayer candles as {player} enters the court.
Sera folds {pronounPossessive} hands and offers {player} a calm greeting.
The temple keeper pauses beside the bells and turns toward {player}.

name: Groundskeeper Orrow
description:
An older groundskeeper in a patched coat tends the graveyard paths with a rake over one shoulder and rosemary tucked into a breast pocket. {pronounPossessiveCap} back is bent by years, but {pronounPossessive} gaze is unflinching.

"The dead ask little. Clean paths, steady names, and someone to notice when the living forget them."
talkTemplates: Orrow brushes moss from a stone. "This place stays honest. Names, dates, and no room for boasting."
"The memorial gets flowers. The graveyard gets visitors after dark," Orrow says. "Both have their reasons."
Orrow nods toward the ruined shrine. "People leave offerings where things are broken. Makes them feel less alone in it."
"If you're looking for peace, tread slower," Orrow advises. "It doesn't care for boots in a hurry."
interactTemplates: Groundskeeper Orrow looks up from a headstone as {player} approaches.
Orrow leans on {pronounPossessive} rake and studies {player} in thoughtful silence.
The graveyard keeper nods once toward {player}.

name: Gatewarden Holt
description:
A square-jawed gatewarden in weathered mail keeps watch beneath the portcullis, one hand resting near the road register and the other near a spear polished smooth by use. The habit of scanning the horizon never seems to leave them.

"Heading out is easy. Coming back with all your pieces is where skill begins."
talkTemplates: Holt glances east. "Quiet roads make me nervous. They usually mean the trouble's learning patience."
"Sign out if you're leaving town," Holt says. "Saves Captain Hale from guessing where to send a search."
Holt taps the gate arch. "Walls aren't for keeping brave people in. They're for giving scared people somewhere to run back to."
"The watchtower sees far, but not far enough to save fools from themselves," Holt says dryly.
interactTemplates: Gatewarden Holt shifts beneath the arch as {player} nears the gate.
Holt glances from the road back to {player} with practiced caution.
The gatewarden gives {player} a brief nod without taking {pronounPossessive} eyes off the frontier for long.

name: Blind Guide
description:
An elderly figure sits cross-legged at the fork in the path, a worn walking staff across {pronounPossessive} knees. {pronounPossessiveCap} eyes are milky and sightless, yet they still seem to find you unerringly as you approach.

"Lost?" comes the question with a knowing smile. "Many are. The forest tests those who seek its heart. But those who follow loyalty's path always find their way."
talkTemplates: The guide tilts {pronounPossessive} head. "The paw marks the faithful. Have you walked beside one who never left your side?"
"The heart knows courage," the guide says. "Did you offer aid when you could have walked past?"
"Joy is often forgotten by those who think themselves too important for play." The guide chuckles softly.
"The compass points true for those who know their purpose. Why do you seek the grove?"
The guide nods slowly. "Four fragments. Four lessons. United, they form something greater."
"East lies the crossing," the guide whispers. "But only the worthy see the path."
interactTemplates: The blind guide turns toward {player}, somehow sensing their presence.
The guide's sightless eyes seem to look through {player}.
The guide inclines {pronounPossessive} head as {player} approaches the fork.
The guide smiles knowingly as {player} arrives.

name: Forest Wolf
description: A lean grey wolf with hungry yellow eyes and scarred muzzle. {pronounPossessiveCap} lips curl back to reveal yellowed fangs, a low growl rumbling from deep in {pronounPossessive} chest. This predator has clearly survived many battles.
talkTemplates: The wolf snarls in response, clearly uninterested in conversation.
A guttural growl is the only reply. Wolves don't talk.
The wolf bares its fangs. Words mean nothing to it.
The wolf's yellow eyes narrow. It understands only one language: violence.
interactTemplates: The forest wolf circles {player}, hackles raised.
The wolf's ears flatten against its skull as {player} approaches.
A threatening growl rumbles from the wolf as it notices {player}.
The wolf tracks {player}'s movements with predatory focus.
